task.title: Configuring an EDS Output Preset
task.shortdesc: This task explains how to configure an Edge Delivery Service output preset on a DITA map in AEM Guides, so generated content is published to a GitHub repository served by aem.live.

ul.li: You have a GitHub repository created from the aem-guides-boilerplate template.
AEM Code Sync is installed on that repository.
The microservice OSGi configurations are deployed to your AEMaaCS environment.
You have administrator permissions in AEM Guides.

context.p: An EDS output preset connects a DITA map to a publish profile, which holds the GitHub credentials that AEM Guides uses to commit generated HTML to your repository. Once configured, clicking Generate Output triggers the full publishing pipeline.

step.cmd: Open your DITA map in Map Console.
step.info: You can access Map Console from the asset browser by right-clicking a map and selecting Open in Map Console.

step.cmd: Navigate to the Output Presets tab.
Click the + icon to create a new preset.
In the preset creation dialog, configure the following options:

choices.choice: Name: enter a descriptive name such as EDS – production.

step.cmd: Click Add to create the preset.
step.stepresult: The new preset appears in your map's preset list.

step.cmd: Open the newly created preset and switch to the Config tab.

substep.cmd: Select your publish profile from the Publish profile dropdown.
Enable the Push to live toggle.

step.cmd: Click Generate Output to trigger the publishing pipeline.
step.info: The microservice converts your DITA content and commits it to the configured GitHub repository under /docs/.

result.p: Your DITA content is now published to GitHub and available on both the EDS preview and live URLs:
result.note: Use the preview URL to validate changes before they go live to end users.

example.title: Example publish profile JSON
example.p: For reference, here is what a complete publish profile looks like when stored in AEM:

postreq.p: After completing this task, see Edge Delivery Services Overview for background on the EDS architecture.
